questions.question: Within an evolutionary strategies algorithm, the mutation operation: 1. Is used only in constrained problems; 2. Is performed as soon as a population of offspring is available; 3. Selects on average half of the individuals for mutation; 4. Uses two distinct multisets of offspring; 5. Is non-uniform type (fluaj); 6. Is performed only once, after the first stage of generating a population; 7. Is performed with low probability; 8. Is performed only once during an algorithm; 9. Determines the chromosomal structure; 10. Uses the current population; 11. Is performed iteratively.

The components of evolutionary algorithms are: 1. Representation; 2. Mutation probability; 3. Fitness function; 4. Recombination probability; 5. Population; 6. Random number generation; 7. Parent selection mechanism; 8. Permutation generation; 9. Graphical representation of quality evolution; 10. Variation operators; 11. Establishing genetic diversity of the population; 12. Current population replacement mechanism; 13. Hillclimbing; 14. Population initialization; 15. Termination condition.
In genetic algorithms, representation via integer arrays:

answers.d: Is preferable when more than two distinct values are possible for each gene

Data validation problems; 6. Simulation problems; 7. Dynamic memory allocation in the computer; 8. Autonomous vehicle movement.

Within the evolutionary strategy with 2 members (ES-2M): 1. An initial random population is generated from the normal distribution; 2. At every stage of the algorithm's evolution, only a single solution candidate is maintained; 3. Populations are large; 4. Calculation of a new term is performed via Gaussian mutation for every component of the current vector; 5. Continuous space problems are solved; 6. Each term is calculated stochastically; 7.A new term is calculated via Gaussian mutation for a randomly selected component of the current vector; 8. Increasing the average quality of the current population is intended; 9. Obtaining a global optimum is guaranteed; 10. At the beginning of each iteration, the size of the current population is tested; 11. Descendants obtained from the current population through local recombination are created.

answers.b: It is not necessary in genetic algorithms

questions.question: Within a genetic algorithm, the operation of survivor selection: 1. Is applied to the current population; 2. In some variants requires calculating a selection probability distribution; 3. Always uses random factors; 4. Chooses the next generation from individuals available after the mutation operation; 5. The selected individuals are always feasible; 6. Sometimes uses random factors; 7. Ensures the perpetuation of the individual with maximum quality in the current population; 8. Leads to an increase in the average quality of the current population; 9. Guarantees obtaining a generation with superior average quality if it uses age-based selection; 10. Is applied at the beginning of each iteration; 11. Is applied to the descendants obtained from the current population; 12. Is applied to the current population and to the descendants obtained from the current population.

The Hillclimbing algorithm: 1. Is applied to a single point in the search space; 2. Application can be repeated for multiple points to increase performance; 3. Is inspired by mountaineering techniques; 4. Always finds the optimal solution; 5. Sometimes finds the optimal solution; 6. Calculations end when the system temperature becomes zero; 7. Usually finds a local optimum point; 8. Is used only for representation with real number strings.

Within an evolutionary strategies class algorithm, chromosome representation: 1. Can be any of: binary strings, integer strings, real number strings, permutations; 2. Can only be of integer or real number string type; 3. Can only be of real number string type; 4. Does not influence the type of recombination used (discrete/intermediate); 5. Is chosen depending on the problem being solved; 6. Influences the type of recombination used; 7. Has influence on the next generation selection mechanism; 8. Contains both the description of the candidate individual and the parameters that control its evolution.
Let X=[ 6 1 8 10 5 7 9 3 4 2 ] and Y=[ 9 8 7 3 6 1 5 10 4 2] be permutations. Which offspring are generated using the CX operator?

Within an evolutionary algorithm, the initial population: 1. Is generated before the actual evolution starts; 2. Is generated after every evolutionary cycle (which involves performing selection, recombination, mutation, evaluation operations); 3. Is generated at the beginning of every evolutionary cycle; 4. Within an evolutionary algorithm, the termination condition: 1. Is mandatory; 2. Is not indicated; 3. Mandatorily includes, directly or indirectly, the control of the number of iterations (the number of populations generated); 4. Cannot be implemented; 5. Is used only within GA, not within ES.

Within a genetic algorithm that maximizes a function (fitness): 1. The selection of the next generation is mandatorily based on age; 2. Only constrained problems are solved; 3.
